novel_features.name: Compaction-aware frame extract for codegen prompts
novel_features.description: Extract a single frame as a compact codegen-ready payload that fuses simplified node tree, in-scope variables, dev resources, and Code Connect mappings.
novel_features.rationale: GLips's compaction pipeline ships only as a Node MCP. No surveyed tool fuses node tree + variables + dev-resources + Code Connect into one payload, accepts both 1234:5678 and 1234-5678, and resolves instance-override chains like I5666:180910;1:10515.
novel_features.why_it_matters: First call when an AI agent needs Figma frame context for code generation — returns a compact payload that fits in the context window instead of the raw 10MB file response.

novel_features.name: Dev-mode resource bundle for a single node
novel_features.description: Emit a portable Markdown bundle that fuses dev-resource links, variables in scope, render permalink, and Code Connect mapping for one node.
novel_features.rationale: Official Dev Mode MCP is closed-source and Desktop-bound. No surveyed tool emits a portable bundle for a single node from the REST surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent or engineer needs the full Dev Mode context for one frame as a single Markdown blob — no Desktop pairing required.

novel_features.name: Cross-file unresolved comments audit
novel_features.description: Aggregate unresolved comments across every synced team file with age and group-by filters.
novel_features.rationale: figma comments list is per-file. No surveyed tool aggregates across team files, and resolution-via-API doesn't exist server-side.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this on Monday morning before design review — surfaces every stale unresolved thread across the team.

novel_features.name: Stale component, style, and variable orphans finder
novel_features.description: Find published library entities (components, styles, variables) with zero usage over a window by joining team-library publish list with library-analytics usage data.
novel_features.rationale: Library analytics is per-entity-per-file in the UI. No surveyed tool joins the publish list with usage data across an entire team library.
novel_features.why_it_matters: First command for the quarterly design-system cleanup — returns the list of entities safe to deprecate.

novel_features.name: Semantic tokens diff between file versions
novel_features.description: Diff Figma variables across two file versions with mode-awareness; emits a Markdown or JSON change set.
novel_features.rationale: figmagic and figmage emit current state only. Tokens Studio diffs Git, not Figma. No surveyed tool diffs variables across file versions.
novel_features.example: figma-pp-cli tokens diff abc123 --from v1.0.0 --to HEAD --format md
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before merging a design-tokens PR to see what actually changed in Figma since the last release.

novel_features.name: Deterministic file fingerprint for CI contract
novel_features.description: Stable hash of a Figma file's token + component + style surface; exits non-zero if --expect doesn't match.
novel_features.rationale: No surveyed tool offers a deterministic contract-check fingerprint. figmagic emits artifacts but not an exit code suitable for CI gating.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Wire this into CI to fail builds when the upstream Figma file's design-system surface drifts from the committed snapshot.

novel_features.name: Webhook delivery replay against new target
novel_features.description: Pull Figma's webhook request log and replay stored payloads (with original headers and HMAC) against an arbitrary target URL.
novel_features.rationale: No surveyed tool replays Figma webhook deliveries. Brief identifies this as Persona D's #1 pain point.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when iterating on a new webhook handler — replay yesterday's failed deliveries against your local server without re-triggering upstream events.

novel_features.name: Variable usage tracer
novel_features.description: Flat list of every node and component that references a given variable across a file.
novel_features.rationale: Figma's UI shows variable references per-node modally. No surveyed tool emits a flat cross-file list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: First call when planning a variable rename or deprecation — shows the blast radius before you touch anything.

narrative.headline: Every Figma endpoint, plus codegen-ready frame extracts, comments audit, orphans finder, tokens diff, and webhook replay no other Figma tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI is the offline-first, agent-native Figma operator. It absorbs every REST endpoint as one Cobra command, ports GLips's compaction pipeline so frame extracts fit in an LLM context window, and adds the analytical primitives no other tool unifies: cross-file comments audit, stale-component orphans finder, semantic tokens diff between file versions, deterministic file fingerprint for CI, and webhook delivery replay.
narrative.auth_narrative: Figma supports two auth modes: Personal Access Token (header X-Figma-Token, prefix figd_) for personal/automation use, and OAuth 2.0 (Authorization: Bearer) for /v1/me, /v1/activity_logs, and /v1/developer_logs. Set FIGMA_API_TOKEN (PAT) or FIGMA_OAUTH2 (OAuth Bearer); the CLI auto-routes to the right header. Run `figma-pp-cli auth login` for OAuth, or save a PAT to the config file via `figma-pp-cli auth set-token`. Doctor surfaces X-Figma-Plan-Tier and X-Figma-Rate-Limit-Type from response headers.

quickstart.comment: Check what auth is currently active. Set FIGMA_API_TOKEN=figd_... for PAT, or run `figma-pp-cli auth login --client-id <id>` for OAuth.
Confirm auth + reachability + plan tier; warns on Enterprise endpoints when only PAT is available.
Smoke-check that auth works against a real file key.
Populate the local SQLite store so cross-file commands work offline.
Killer command — emits compact prompt-ready frame context for codegen agents.
Find every unresolved comment older than two weeks across your synced files.

troubleshoots.fix: PATs require the `current_user:read` scope on /v1/me; the auto-routing client uses your PAT for file/comment endpoints (which work) and warns when /v1/me is requested. For full /me coverage run `figma-pp-cli auth login` (OAuth).

Tier-1 endpoints (files/images/nodes) have strict per-plan-tier limits. Use `figma-pp-cli files meta get-file` (Tier-1-light) for routine checks; reserve full file fetches for `sync` runs that respect Retry-After.

troubleshoots.symptom: Image URLs from /v1/files/{key}/images are 403ing after a week
troubleshoots.fix: Image-fill URLs expire 14 days after generation; rendered-node URLs expire 30 days. Re-run `figma-pp-cli files images get-fills` to refresh.

troubleshoots.symptom: Activity logs returns 403 even with admin PAT
troubleshoots.fix: /v1/activity_logs is OAuth-only and Enterprise-tier. Set FIGMA_OAUTH2 to your OAuth Bearer token and ensure your account is on Enterprise.

troubleshoots.symptom: frame extract output is still huge
troubleshoots.fix: Drop --depth (default 4) to a smaller number, or restrict --ids to the specific node you need. The compaction pipeline reports `simplifiedNodeCount` so you can see compression.

narrative.when_to_use: Use figma-pp-cli when an agent or engineer needs to reason about a Figma file as data — not pixels. It shines for codegen prompt context (`frame extract`, `dev-mode dump`), design-system ops (`orphans`, `tokens diff`, `fingerprint`), comment hygiene (`comments audit`), and webhook iteration (`webhooks test`). For interactive design work, the Figma desktop app is fine — figma-pp-cli covers everything Figma's REST API exposes and adds the cross-file analytical commands the dashboard hides.

recipes.title: Extract a frame for an AI codegen prompt
recipes.explanation: The killer command. Returns a compact JSON with the simplified node tree, every variable in scope, every dev-resource link, and a node count showing the compression ratio — paste it directly into Claude or Cursor.

recipes.title: Find unresolved comments older than two weeks
recipes.explanation: After sync, walks the comments table and aggregates by file and author. Pipe through jq for Slack-ready Markdown.

recipes.title: Cleanup orphans across a team library
recipes.explanation: Joins team-library publish list with 30-day usage analytics; emits the list of entities published but with zero usages — your deprecation candidates. Enterprise-tier required.

recipes.title: Diff design tokens between two file versions
recipes.command: figma-pp-cli tokens diff abc123 --from v1.0.0 --to HEAD --format md
recipes.explanation: Resolves the version ids, snapshots variables at each, and emits a Markdown change-set listing added/removed/renamed/value-changed tokens. Run before merging a design-tokens PR.

recipes.title: Replay failed webhook deliveries against a local server
recipes.explanation: Fetches the request log via /v2/webhooks/{id}/requests, filters status >= 400, and replays each delivery against your local URL with the captured payload and original headers. (HMAC re-signing is not yet wired — your handler must accept unsigned replays for testing.)

trigger_phrases: extract a figma frame for codegen
compact figma file context for AI
where is this figma variable used
